name: vwan
title: Azure Transit — Virtual WAN
summary: Creates a Virtual WAN with one or more virtual hubs (per region), VNet-to-hub connections with full routing configuration (association, propagation including propagate-to-none isolation, static next-hop-IP routes), custom hub route tables, static hub routes (custom **or default** route table), hub-router BGP peerings with NVAs in connected VNets, and a CanNotDelete management lock (on by default).

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: resource_group_name
module.description: Name of the existing resource group for the virtual WAN.

module.name: location
module.description: Azure region for the virtual WAN resource itself.

module.name: wan_type
module.description: Virtual WAN type: 'Standard' (full transit) or 'Basic' (S2S VPN only).

module.name: hubs
module.type: map(object({ location = string address_prefix = string hub_routing_preference = optional(string, "ExpressRoute") }))
module.description: Virtual hubs keyed by logical name, one per region. Address prefix must not overlap any connected VNet or branch.

module.name: vnet_connections
module.type: map(object({ hub_key = string vnet_id = string internet_security_enabled = optional(bool, false) associated_route_table_key = optional(string) propagate_none = optional(bool, false) propagated_route_table_keys = optional(list(string), []) propagated_labels = optional(list(string), []) static_routes = optional(map(object({ address_prefixes = list(string) next_hop_ip_address = string })), {}) }))
module.description: VNet-to-hub connections keyed by logical name, referencing hubs by logical key. Spoke VNet IDs may come from any source. propagate_none and propagated_route_table_keys/labels are mutually exclusive per connection.

module.name: hub_route_tables
module.description: Custom hub route tables keyed by logical name (e.g., 'spokes'). Routes are defined separately in hub_routes to keep the resource graph acyclic (route tables <- connections <- routes).

module.name: hub_routes
module.type: map(object({ hub_key = string route_table_key = optional(string) destinations = list(string) destinations_type = optional(string, "CIDR") next_hop_connection_key = string }))
module.description: Static hub routes keyed by logical name, targeting a custom route table (route_table_key) or the hub's default route table (route_table_key = null — required for steering BRANCH traffic, since VPN/ER connections can only associate with the default route table). next_hop is always a VNet connection (next_hop_type ResourceId) — the firewall services VNet connection in the NVA pattern.

module.name: bgp_connections
module.type: map(object({ hub_key = string peer_asn = number peer_ip = string connection_key = optional(string) }))
module.description: Hub router BGP peerings with NVAs in connected VNets (e.g., Palo Alto VM-Series), keyed by logical name. Lets the NVA advertise routes dynamically instead of relying on static hub_routes. Requires a Standard WAN.

module.name: enable_management_lock
module.description: Apply a CanNotDelete management lock to the virtual WAN (governance/resource-locks).

outputs.name: hub_id
outputs.description: Map of logical hub name to virtual hub ID.

outputs.name: hub_default_route_table_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical hub name to the hub's default route table ID (branch connections always associate here).

outputs.name: route_table_ids
outputs.description: Map of custom hub route table IDs keyed by logical name, plus 'default/<hub-key>' entries for each hub's default route table.

outputs.name: attachment_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical connection name to virtual hub connection ID. Use these as next_hop_connection_key targets and as gateway attachment references.

outputs.name: hub_route_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical route name to hub route resource ID.

outputs.name: bgp_connection_ids
outputs.description: Map of logical BGP peering name to hub BGP connection ID.
